S-Bahn: S5, S7, or S75 to Hackescher Markt | phone=+49 20 2026-9136 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 09:00–20:00, Su and holidays 12:00–20:00 | price=€7, concessions €5 | wikipedia=Berlin Cathedral | image=View from Humboldtbox - Berlin Cathedral.jpg | wikidata=Q154563 | content=The city's Protestant cathedral and the burial place of the Prussian kings. You can climb to the top and get a view of the city. }}
* {{see | name=Stadtschloss – Humboldt Forum | alt=Berlin City Palace | url=https://www.humboldtforum.org/en/ | email= | address= | lat=52.5175 | long=13.402778 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=City Palace, Berlin | image=Berlin Stadtschloss um 1900.jpg | wikidata=Q170119 | content=Started in the 15th century and finished in the mid-18th century, the baroque palace was the residence of electors, kings and emperors until 1918, when it became a museum. The palace was badly damaged during World War II and later razed in 1950, replaced by the GDR with a modernist ''Palast der Republik''. The ''Palast'' was in turn gradually dismantled at the turn of the century, as it was discovered to contain asbestos and its former function of housing the GDR parliament became obsolete. Berlin has started in June 2013 construction on a new version of its historic Stadtschloss. The Schlüterhof, an inner courtyard, was also rebuilt. The building opened with a delay in 2021 with museums inside and a roof terrace with a good view. Among the Berlin museums this is perhaps the most controversial due to reconstruction of a monarchist palace being seen as a questionable political statement and due to the fact that many of the exhibits were sourced from German colonies under ethically questionable circumstances leading to demands to return some or all of them to their places of origin. }}
===Alexanderplatz and Alt-Berlin === [[File:Berliner Fernsehturm TV Tower.jpg|thumb|175px|The Fernsehturm]] The square used to host a cattle market (''Ochsenplatz''). It was named after the Russian Tsar Alexander I when he visited in 1805. It rose to prominence in the 19th century following the construction of a railway and gradually became the eastern focal point of Berlin. The bustling area around the square was immortalized by Alfred Döblin in a monumental novel ''Berlin Alexanderplatz'' (1929). The novel has been adapted into film twice, with the newer adaptation the 1980 Rainer Werner Fassbinder 14-hour behemoth being the better known by far.
The Alexanderplatz area was largely destroyed during the Second World War and redeveloped by socialist city planners as the new centre of East Berlin. The vast expanses of open spaces and large, imposing examples of modern architecture provide for a very different feel than the part of Mitte located across the Spree. Incidentally, the areas directly southwest of modern-day Alexanderplatz were the places where the city of Berlin originates from (''Alt-Berlin''), and many remains of that can be found interspersed between the modern architecture of the quarter.
Two of the highest buildings in Berlin, the ''Fernsehturm'' and the Park Inn hotel, dominate today's Alexanderplatz, while historic buildings such as the ''Rotes Rathaus'', ''Marienkirche'' and the ''Nikolaiviertel'' flank its sides.
* {{see | name=Fernsehturm | alt=Television tower | url=http://www.tv-turm.de/en | email= | address=Panoramastraße 1A | lat=52.520833 | long=13.409444 | directions=S-Bahn and U-Bahn Alexanderplatz | phone=+49 30 24 75 75 37 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Mar–Oct 09:00–00:00, Nov–Feb 10:00–00:00 | price=€13, children €8.50 (if prebooked, slightly more walk up). Discount with voucher from other attractions | wikipedia=Fernsehturm Berlin | image=Berliner_Fernsehturm_-_von_unten.jpg | wikidata=Q151356 |lastedit=2020-08-11| content=At 368 metres, the Fernsehturm is Berlin's tallest and indeed EU's second-tallest building, complected between 1965 and 1969. The main function of the building is television broadcasting, but the shiny sphere atop the tower houses a viewing platform, a restaurant and a Berlin Tourist Information point. The viewing platform sits 203 metres above ground, affording views of as much as 42 km away and featuring a bar. Given how flat Berlin is, it's not a very interesting view however and the state of the glass means that you can't get good photos either. The ''Sphere'' restaurant at 207 metres rotates at a speed of one full spin per 30 minutes. There are two lifts from the base to the platform and cafe, taking 40 seconds to reach the top, as well as a 986-step staircase. The ''Fernsehturm'' is not accessible to wheelchair users.<br>Not all of the Berliners liked the tower and the overall composition of the Alexanderplatz afforded by the socialist city planners. East German T.V. tried to establish the nickname "Telespargel" ("television asparagus") which however doesn't seem to have caught on. During certain times of day, sunlight reflecting from the top caused a large cross-shaped light to shine down on the city. Called the ''Rache des Papstes'' (Pope's revenge) by nominally atheist East Berliners, the light-cross was an ironic result of socialist architecture. Rumour has it the architect was deprived of more than his next commission after that fiasco. At night, the Fernsehturm sometimes appears to be shooting light beams from the tower section, giving the impression it's a Death Star à la Star Wars. }} * {{see | name=Weltzeituhr | alt=Urania World Clock | url= | email= | address=Alexanderplatz | lat=52.52116 | long=13.41331 | directions=U-Bahn & S-Bahn: Alexanderplatz | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Weltzeituhr (Alexanderplatz) | image=Urania-Weltzeituhr auf dem Alexanderplatz in Berlin 2015.jpg | wikidata=Q186173 | content=Built in 1969, this 16-ton, communist-era clock is one of Berlin's main meeting points. Each of its 24 sides corresponds to one of Earth's 24 time zones and it has the names of some of the world's most important cities written on it. }} * {{see | name=Neptunbrunnen | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.519722 | long=13.406667 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Neptunbrunnen | image=Neptun 1a 2.jpg | wikidata=Q650657 | content=A bronze fountain by Reinhold Begas. It was erected in 1891 as a present from the city of Berlin to the Kaiser. Neptune, trident in hand, presides over the square supported by sea-nymphs with webbed feet carrying him on a seashell. Denizens of the deep (a seal, an alligator, snakes and turtles, among others) spray water at him in homage while languishing mermaids pour water into the fountain, clutching sea-nets overflowing with marine bounty. }} * [[File:Berlin Aussichten vom Dom asv2023-02 img01.jpg|thumb|Rotes Rathaus]]{{see | name=Rotes Rathaus | alt=Red City Hall | url=http://www.berlin.de/en/attractions-and-sights/3559880-3104052-red-town-hall.en.html | email= | address= | lat=52.518611 | long=13.408333 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Rotes Rathaus | image=Berlin Rotes Rathaus 2.jpg | wikidata=Q155210 | content=The town hall of Berlin is known as such because it is made of red brick, not due to its former political persuasion. There are nice Prussian rooms inside, which are worth a look. }} * {{see | name=Marienkirche | alt=St. Mary's Church | url= | email= | address=Karl-Liebknecht-Straße | lat=52.520556 | long=13.406667 | directions=next to the Fernsehturm | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=St. Mary's Church, Berlin | image=Berlín, Mitte, kostel Marienkirche a Neptunova kašna.jpg | wikidata=Q679101 | content=Gothic church, the second oldest (built in late 13th century) of the historical centre of Berlin. It's the highest church tower of Berlin (about 90 m), but seems rather small beneath the gigantic TV tower. The church tower was built in the late 18th century by Carl Gotthard Langhans, the architect of the Brandenburg Gate. }}
[[File:Alt-Berlin Nikolaikirche Berlin-Mitte Abend.jpg|thumb|175px|Church of St. Nicholas]] * {{see | name=Nikolaikirche | alt=St. Nicholas Church | url=http://www.stadtmuseum.de/nikolaikirche | email= | address=Nikolaikirchplatz | lat=52.51678 | long=13.40698 | directions= | phone=+49 30 24 002-162 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=daily 10:00–18:00 | price=€5 | wikipedia=St. Nicholas' Church, Berlin | image=Nikolaikirche Berlin April 2007.jpg | wikidata=Q679052 | content=Berlin's oldest church (1230) is a 3-nave hall church. It is in the centre of an area destroyed by bombs in the war which was then turned into a faux "old town" by the East German authorities called Nikolaiviertel. The area is more a hodge-podge of relocated buildings than an authentic reproduction, and the newly-built 1988 apartments that attempt to "harmonize" with the older buildings are embarrassing. The church is one of the only structures that was renovated rather than rebuilt. It is best known for a sandstone sculpture called the Spandauer Madonna (1290), but there are other interesting pieces here. When the church was destroyed in 1938 and rebuilt in the 1970s, the communist officials intended to use it as a museum, which did not open until 1987. The museum includes sacred textiles and religious sculpture from the fourteenth to the sixteenth centuries. The Nikolaikirche is the showplace of the Nikolaiviertel, which isn't saying much. }}
[[File:Mitte Nikolaikirchplatz-001.jpg|thumb|Nikolaiviertel]] * {{see | name=Nikolaiviertel | alt= | url=https://berlin-nikolaiviertel.com/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | image=Mitte Nikolaikirchplatz-001.jpg | wikidata=Q703927 | content= }} * {{see | name=Zille Museum | alt= | url=http://zillemuseum-berlin.de/ | email= | address=Propststraße 11 | lat=52.51643 | long=13.40613 | directions= | phone=+49 30 246 32 502 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | image=Zille-Museum Nikolaiviertel - Berlin 2013 - 1384-1264-120.jpg | content=A museum in Nikolaiviertel dedicated to the Berliner artist Heinrich Zille. }} * {{see | name=Hanfmuseum | alt=Hemp Museum | url=http://www.hanfmuseum.de | email=info@hanfmuseum.de | address=Mühlendamm 5 | lat=52.5163791 | long=13.4080074 | directions=Bus M48, Station 'Nikolaiviertel', everything else near Alexanderplatz | phone=+49 30 242 48 27 | tollfree= | hours=Tu–F 10:00–20:00, Sa Su 12:00–20:00, M closed | price=€4.50, concession €3 | wikipedia=Hemp Museum (Berlin) | image=Hanfmuseum1.jpg | wikidata=Q1575394 | content=It is the only hemp museum in Germany; you can see the history of hemp, the culture and use of it. You can see hemp grow. There is a cafe downstairs, with an open WiFi access. Everything going on here is legal - including the hemp growing under artificial light (a low THC strain grown with a special permit) - but they do not refrain from political commentary on the legal situation of [[cannabis]] in their exhibits. }} * {{see | name=DDR Museum | alt= | url=http://www.ddr-museum.de/en | email=post@ddr-museum.de | address=Karl-Liebknecht-Straße 1 | lat=52.51944 | long=13.40222 | directions=across from Berliner Dom on the banks of River Spree | phone=+49 30 847 123 73-1, +49 30 847 123 73-0 | tollfree= | fax=+49 30 847 123 73-9 | hours=Su–F 10:00–20:00, Sa 10:00–22:00 | price=€7, concession €4 | wikipedia=DDR Museum | image=DDR-Museum Berlin Wohnzimmer 01.jpg | wikidata=Q573322 | content=A museum dedicated to everyday life in communist East Germany. The museum has very relaxed rules and you are allowed to touch and examine almost every object, which adds greatly to the experience. }} * {{see | name=Volkspark am Weinberg | alt=Weinbergspark | url=https://www.visitberlin.de/en/volkspark-am-weinberg | email= | address= | lat=52.532125 | long=13.400914 | directions=U-Bahn: U-Bahn: U8 Rosenthaler Platz, Tram: 12,M8 Brunnenstr./Invalidenstr., M1 Rosenthaler Platz | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | wikipedia=de:Volkspark_am_Weinberg | image=MitteVolksparkAmWeinberg-4.jpg | wikidata=Q1648437 | content=This sloping public park is very popular among locals for sunbathing, relaxing, having a picnic or playing the guitar. }} * {{see | name=Park Inn | alt=Panorama Terasse | url=http://www.parkinn-berlin.de/en/ | email= | address= | lat=52.522778 | long=13.412778 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00–18:00 | price=€4 | wikipedia=Park Inn Berlin | image=Berlin - Park Inn Hotel1.jpg | wikidata=Q701571 | content=Small terrace on the top of the Park Inn, publicly accessible. Take the elevator to the 40th floor, and follow the signs up the stairs. Pay the attendant who also serves beer and coffee. Great views of the Fernsehturm. In the summer, consider '''base jumping''' off the roof with Jochen Schweizer. It is often closed in bad/windy weather, so look for a notice posted near the elevator that the terrace is closed. }}
===Spreebogen and Regierungsviertel=== {{Infobox|Reichstag or Bundestag?|You may be confused to find the large building with the glass dome referred to by two different names. ''Reichstag'' (short for ''Reichstagsgebäude'', Reichstag building) refers to the building itself, while ''Bundestag'' is the name of the legislative body that meets there – the German parliament. In everyday speech, Germans don't always make that distinction and sometimes refer to the building as ''Bundestag'' (even the U-Bahn stop is called that), but never the other way around: the last people to refer to the parliament as ''Reichstag'' were the Nazis. As a visitor, feel free to use both terms for the building, you will be understood.}} [[File:Reichstag Berlin-Tiergarten.jpg|thumb|250px|Outside of the Reichstag Dome]] [[File:Berlin Kongresshalle „Schwangere Auster“.jpg|thumb|250px|House of the Cultures of the World]] * {{see | name=Regierungsviertel/Spreebogen | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.51971 | long=13.37178 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Regierungsviertel_(Berlin) | image=Regierungsviertel, Berlin-Mitte, Blaue Stunde, Panorama, 160206, ako.jpg | wikidata=Q1683614 | content=The area to the north of Tiergarten, along the bow of the river Spree (''Spreebogen''), is home to the German federal institutions such as the parliament (''Bundestag'', in the historic ''Reichstag'' building) and the federal government, as well as the new central train station (''Hauptbahnhof'') across the river. }} * {{see | name=Reichstagsgebäude | alt=Reichstag building | url=http://www.bundestag.de/en | email= | address=Platz der Republik 1 | lat=52.5186 | long=13.3754 | directions={{rint|berlin|u55}} Bundestag | phone=+49 30 227 0 | tollfree= | hours=8:00-24:00 | price=Free | wikipedia=Reichstag building | image=Reichstagsgebäude mit Weihnachtsbaum bei Nacht, Berlin, 151223, ako.jpg | wikidata=Q151897 | lastedit=2025-06-16 | content=This imposing building houses the Federal German Parliament or "Bundestag" and was completed in 1894 to meet the need of the newly-unified German Empire of the Kaisers for a larger parliamentary building. The Reichstag was intended to resemble a Renaissance palace, and its architect, Paul Wallot, dedicated the building to the German people. The massive inscription in front still reads: "Dem Deutschen Volke" - 'For the German people'. The Nazi leader Adolf Hitler exploited the fire which gutted the Reichstag building in 1933 by blaming the Communists for the arson and for attempted revolution. There is good evidence to suggest, however, that his followers were actually responsible and that this was a manufactured crisis. The iconic photo symbolizing the Soviet victory over Nazi Germany shows a Soviet soldier planting the Soviet flag on top of the building and there are to this day graffiti left by Soviet soldiers on some walls of the Reichstag which were deliberately preserved by the new Germany as a memento of the war. It's perhaps the only national parliament to have traces left by a foreign army deliberately preserved. When German reunification became a reality, the new republic was proclaimed here at midnight on 2 October 1990. The Reichstag building is well-known in the art world thanks to Paris-based Bulgarian artist Christo's mammoth 'Wrapped Reichstag' project in 1995. The entire building was swathed in silver cloth for two weeks that summer.<br />The Reichstag has undergone considerable restoration and alteration, including the addition of a spectacular glass dome designed by the British architect Norman Foster completed in 1999. You can visit the Reichstag building proper and even listen to a parliamentary debate but you need to book on their website sometimes weeks or even months in advance. Fortunately its much easier to visit the glass dome. You can reserve a visiting time and date on their website or in the small building across Scheidmannstrasse, except during the high season you should be able to arrange a time later the same day or the next day. Photo ID or passport is required to make the booking. A passport is required during your visit. This is a very popular tourist attraction in Berlin and can get quite crowded however it is worth the effort. The helical path up the inside of the dome is a lot of fun and the 360 degree views at the top are splendid. }} * {{see | name=Bundeskanzleramt | alt=Federal Chancellery | url=http://www.bundeskanzleramt.de/ | email= | address=Willy-Brandt-Straße 1 | lat=52.5202 | long=13.3692 | directions= | phone=+49 30 182722720 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Federal Chancellery (Germany) | image=Kanzler21a.jpg | wikidata=Q317027 | lastedit=2025-06-16 | content=The building houses the personal offices of the Chancellor and the Chancellery staff. The Berlin Chancellery is one of the largest government headquarters buildings in the world. By comparison, the new Chancellery building is ten times the size of the White House. A semi official Chancellor's apartment is on the top floor of the building. The 200-m², two-room flat has thus far only been occupied by Gerhard Schröder chancellors since then have lived elsewhere. It is usually not possible to visit the building, but on occasion there are tours, usually around August. The building was deliberately designed in a way to symbolize the German constitutional system - it's in the line of sight of the ''Bundestag'' and lower in height, symbolizing the role of parliament in controlling government and "the people's house" being the higher power in the relationship between the two. Or at least that's the idea. }} * {{see | name=Kongresshalle – Haus der Kulturen der Welt | alt=House of the Cultures of the World | url=http://www.hkw.de/en/ | email= | address= John-Foster-Dulles-Allee | lat=52.51885 | long=13.36489 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=W–M 11:00–19:00 | price=Around €8 depending on exhibit | wikipedia=de:Kongresshalle_(Berlin) | image=Berlin_Kongresshalle_„Schwangere_Auster“.jpg | wikidata=Q895566 | content=Germany's national centre for contemporary non-European art. The house is a leading centre for the contemporary arts and a venue for projects breaking through artistic boundaries. This architectural landmark was an American contribution to the international building exhibition INTERBAU 1957 as an embodiment of the free exchange of ideas. Colloquially called ''Schwangere Auster'' (Pregnant Oyster). }} * {{see | name= | alt=BMM | url=https://www.bmm-charite.de/ | email=bmm@charite.de | address=Charitéplatz 1 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+49 30 450536156 | tollfree= | hours=Tu Th F Su 10:00-17:00; W Sa 10:00-19:00 (closed probably until October 2021) | price=€9, concession €4 | wikipedia=de:Berliner_Medizinhistorisches_Museum | wikidata=Q821643 | lastedit=2019-11-04 | content=Interesting exhibition charting the development of European hospitals from the 14th century to the present day. }}
=== Tiergarten === [[File:Berlin - Siegessäule Großer Tiergarten 2012.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Siegessäule on Großer Stern]] [[File:Berlin Elefantentor 09-2017.jpg|thumb|250px|Elephant Gate Zoological Garden]] * {{see | name=Aquarium | alt= | url=http://www.aquarium-berlin.de/en | email= | address=Budapester Straße 32 | lat=52.505833 | long=13.340556 | directions= | phone=+49 30 254010 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00-18:00 | price=Adult €15.50, student €10.50, family ticket €41 | wikipedia=Aquarium Berlin | image=Zoo Berlin Aquarium Fassade 3.jpg | wikidata=Q317985 | lastedit=2018-08-24 | content=The largest aquarium in Germany with over 9000 animals that are presented on three storeys in a historic building. Aquarium Berlin is found on the premises of the Zoo, but can also be visited separately. One of the best places on a rainy day with children. }} * {{see | name=Bauhaus-Archiv / Museum für Gestaltung | alt= | url=http://www.bauhaus.de/ | email= | address=Klingelhöferstraße 14 | lat=52.5063451 | long=13.3539123 | directions= | phone=+49 30 2540020 | tollfree= | hours=W-M 10:00-17:00 | price=Sa-M €7/€4; W-F €6/€3 | wikipedia=Bauhaus Archive | image=Bauhaus archiv berlijn.JPG | wikidata=Q811389 | lastedit=2019-10-26 | content='''Closed''' for renovations; the temporary Bauhaus-Archiv is at Knesebeckstraße 1-2 in [[Charlottenburg]]. Building designed by Walter Gropius. Inside a museum, library, cafe and shop. }} * {{see | name=Bendlerblock | alt= | url=http://www.gdw-berlin.de/en/home/ | email= | address=Stauffenbergstraße 13 - 14 | lat=52.5069 | long=13.3614 | directions=Entrance is through the commemorative courtyard | phone=+49 30 26 99 50 00 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-W F O9:00-18:00, Th 09:00-20:00, Sa Su and holidays 10:00-18:00 | price=Admission and guided tours are free of charge | wikipedia=Bendlerblock | image=Berlin, Tiergarten, Reichpietschufer, Bendler-Block 02.jpg | wikidata=Q695840 | lastedit=2016-01-24 | content=The Bendlerblock building complex has long held ties to the German military, first serving as the offices of the Imperial German Navy and today housing the Berlin offices of the Ministry of Defense. It was here where, on 20 July 1944, Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg and other officers led a coup that sought to remove Hitler and the Nazis from power. They failed and were summarily executed in the courtyard, where a memorial stands for these men who are considered German heroes by many. Inside the building you'll find the German Resistance Memorial Center, a permanent exhibit dedicated to the July 20 plot and other individuals in the German resistance. }} * {{see | name=Berlin Musical Instrument Museum | alt= | url=https://simpk.de/mim_3.html | email= | address=Tiergartenstraße 1 | lat=52.5103 | long=13.3709 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q567069 | lastedit=2020-08-26 | content=Museum established in 1888, with a collection of 3,500 instruments. }} * {{see | name=Siegessäule | alt=Victory Column | url=https://www.berlin.de/en/attractions-and-sights/3560160-3104052-victory-column.en.html | email= | address=Großer Stern 1 / Straße des 17. Juni | lat=52.51456 | long=13.35004 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Apr-Oct: M-F 09:30-18:30; Nov-Mar: 10:00–17:00. 30 minutes longer on weekends. Closed on December 24 | price=€3, reduced €2.50 (only cash) | wikipedia=Berlin Victory Column | image=Berlin_-_Siegessäule_Großer_Tiergarten_2012.jpg | wikidata=Q154987 | lastedit=2020-01-20 | content=Want to feel like one of the angels in Wim Wenders' classic film Der Himmel über Berlin (a.k.a. Wings of Desire)? Climb to the top of Gold-Else, as the statue of Victory on the top of the Victory Column is known. Just don't jump off if you're not actually an angel. Unfortunately there is no elevator, so be prepared for 285 steps to the platform at 50.7 m.<br /><br />Else was built to commemorate Prussian military prowess in the wars against Denmark (1864), Austria (1866) and France (1870-71), and moved to her present location by the Nazis. Five roads run into a traffic circle called Grosser Stern, in the centre of which is the Siegessäule. Else is visible from much of the city district known as Tiergarten. At the base of the statue are reliefs of war scenes representing the conflicts which this monument memorializes. The Allies forced Germany to take those panels down in 1945, but they were remounted in 1984 and 1987. It also served as a backdrop for a speech by then senator Obama in 2008, after his request to speak in front of Brandenburger Tor caused a political debate in Germany. }} * {{see | name=Schloss Bellevue | alt=Bellevue Palace | url=http://www.bundespraesident.de/EN/OfficialResidences/Schloss-Bellevue/bellevuepalace-node.html | email=besucherdienst@bpra.bund.de (to ask for a tour) | address=Spreeweg 1 | lat=52.5175 | long=13.353333 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= Free | wikipedia=Bellevue Palace (Germany) | image=Bellevue Palace Berlin 02-14.jpg | wikidata=Q317091 | content=Official residence of the (largely ceremonial) President of Germany since 1994. Only Roman Herzog (president 1994-1999) actually lived here, his successors have preferred a quiet apartment on the outskirts of Berlin, but this is where the president will usually host guests and do public events. Guided tours are possible, but plan to book up to nine months ahead and be prepared for having to reschedule if the president decides to hold an event on short notice which preempts tours. }} * {{see | name=Soviet War Memorial | alt=Sowjetisches Ehrenmal | url=https://www.berlin.de/en/attractions-and-sights/3561689-3104052-soviet-war-memorial-in-tiergarten.en.html | email= | address=Straße des 17. Juni 4 | lat=52.5167 | long=13.3722 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=24/7 | price=Free | wikipedia=Soviet War Memorial (Tiergarten) | image=Sowjetisches Ehrenmal (Berlin-Tiergarten) Totale.jpg | wikidata=Q32660126 | lastedit=2017-05-04 | content=One of three war memorials in Berlin erected by the Soviet Union to commemorate its war dead, particularly the 80,000 soldiers of the Soviet Armed Forces who died during the Battle of Berlin in April and May 1945. Contrary to popular belief, it has ''not'' been granted extraterritoriality even though it was guarded by Soviet troops throughout Berlin partition despite being in the British, not the Soviet, sector. It ''is'' however subject to a bilateral treaty between Germany and Russia that mandates Germany take care of the monument and keep it in a good state of repair. }} * {{see | name=Zoological Garden | alt=Zoologischer Garten | url=http://www.zoo-berlin.de/en | email=info@zoo-berlin.de | address=Hardenbergplatz 8 or Budapester Str. | lat=52.50563 | long=13.33973 | directions= | phone=+49 30 25401 0 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00-18:30 | price=€13, Zoo & Aquarium €20, students €10/€15, children 5-15 years €6.50/€10, family ticket: €35/€50, annual tickets available | wikipedia=Berlin Zoological Garden | image=Elefantentor_Berlin_Zoo_Nacht.jpg | wikidata=Q154828 | lastedit=2018-03-15 | content=The largest zoo in the world, both in terms of number of species (1500) and animal population (14,000). It is especially famous for its pandas. The Elephant Gate (Budapester Straße), one of the two entrances and next to the Aquarium, is a traditional photo stop for most visitors because of the architecture. }}
===Spandauer Vorstadt=== [[File:Berlin-Mitte Neue Synagoge Oranienburger Straße.jpg|thumb|New Synagogue]] * {{see | name=Hackesche Höfe | alt= | url=https://www.hackesche-hoefe.de/ | email= | address=Rosenthaler Straße 40 | lat=52.524167 | long=13.402222 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=09:00-20:00 (closed at night) | price= | wikipedia=Hackesche Höfe | image=Hackesche Höfe Berlin1.JPG | wikidata=Q736425 | lastedit=2018-06-09 | content=The complex consists of eight interconnected courtyards. It displays more than 300 unique and original Ramones memorabilia. You can get a drink at cafe Mania inside the museum. DeeDee Ramone grew up in Berlin and the city is mentioned in several of their songs. }}
=== Potsdamer Platz and Kulturforum === [[File:SonyCenterBerlin2009.jpg|thumb|250px|Inside the Sony Center, near Potsdamer Platz]]
The '''Potsdamer Platz''' and the neighbouring Leipziger Platz were important squares in pre-war Berlin, but were almost entirely razed during the Second World War, and in the aftermath they became a strip of no man's land separating East and West Berlin. To bring together the disjointed city, a large-scale project was initiated after the German reunification to fill in the empty space with large, impressive and modern buildings, housing corporate headquarters, commercial and entertainment venues and upscale apartments. Around the turn of the 21st century, Potsdamer Platz was the biggest building site in Europe by some measures. Today, the Potsdamer Platz is a major draw for tourists and a lively hub of Berlin.
[[File:Berlin Leipziger Platz - Festival of Lights.jpg|thumb|250px|Leipziger Platz and Potsdamer Platz]] [[File:Berlin Kammermusiksaal Kulturforum Tiergarten & Sony-Center.jpg|thumb|250px|Kurturforum: Kammermusiksaal]] * {{see | name=Kollhoff Tower | alt= | url=http://www.panoramapunkt.de/en/kollhoff-tower.html | email= | address=Potsdamer Platz 1 | lat=52.50915 | long=13.375012 | directions= | phone=+49 30 25937080 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00–20:00 | price=€6.50, concession €5 | wikipedia=de:Kollhoff-Tower | image=| wikidata=Q1779433 | content=Includes the Panoramapunkt, the viewing terrace located 101 metres above ground, accessible by Europe's fastest elevator. }} * {{see | name=Sony Center | alt= | url=http://www.sonycenter.de/en | email= | address=Potsdamer Platz 1 | lat=52.51 | long=13.373611 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Sony Center | image=SonyCenterAtNight.jpg | wikidata=Q641108 | content=With an impressive, circus-tent-like roof over its courtyard and remains of the pre-war Hotel Esplanade incorporated into the modern structure. }} * {{see | name=Deutschlandmuseum | alt= | url=https://www.deutschlandmuseum.de/en/ | email= | address=Leipziger Platz 7| lat=52.50878 | long=13.37844 | directions= | phone= +49 30 2000 90 300 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Su–SA 10:00–20:00 | price=adults €11–12.50, children €8.00–€15.50 | wikipedia=Deutschlandmuseum | image= | wikidata=Q120122369 | content=An immersive history museum, opened in 2023 }} * {{see | name=Leipziger Platz | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.5097 | long=13.3786 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Leipziger Platz | image=Leipziger Platz Berlin - von oben.jpg | wikidata=Q672806 | content=The octagonal square right east of Potsdamer Platz was recreated to resemble its pre-war layout, but the buildings are modern rather than historic replicas and much taller than their counterparts from before the war. There is a diverse mix of uses among the buildings, which include the Embassy of Canada. }}
Immediately west of Potsdamer Platz begins the '''Kulturforum''', an ensemble of buildings housing cultural institutions built on the outskirts of the former West Berlin, as most of the seats of former cultural institutions of Berlin remained in the East. The buildings of the ''Kulturforum'' represent the various bold styles of architecture of the 1950s and 1960s.
* {{see | name=Kulturforum | alt= | url=http://www.kulturforum-berlin.com/engl_index.html | email= | address=Matthäikirchplatz | lat=52.5083 | long=13.3678 | directions= | phone=+49 171 6267864 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Kulturforum | image=Staatsbibliothek zu Berlin Haus 2.jpg | wikidata=Q386482 | content=A collection of most important and architecturally impressive cultural institutions, including many museums and galleries, that was built in West Berlin next to the wall separating it from the Berlin historic centre, which remained in the East along with the original cultural institutions of Berlin. }} * {{see | name=Neue Staatsbibliothek - Haus Potsdamer Straße | alt=Berlin State Library - House Potsdamer Straße | url=http://staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/die-staatsbibliothek/die-gebaeude/potsdamer-strasse/ | email= | address=Potsdamer Straße 33 | lat=52.5075 | long=13.3707 | directions= | phone=+49 30 266433888 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Staatsbibliothek_zu_Berlin_(Haus_Potsdamer_Straße) | image=Staatsbibliothek zu Berlin Haus 2.jpg | wikidata=Q1675021 | content=Designed by Hans Scharoun. }} * {{see | name=Gemäldegalerie | alt=Old Master Paintings | url=http://www.smb.museum/gg | email= | address=Matthäikirchplatz (Stauffenbergstraße 40) | lat=52.50861 | long=13.36472 | directions= | phone=+49 30-2662101 | tollfree= | fax=+49 30-2662103 | hours=Tu W F 10:00–18:00, Th 10:00–20:00, Sa Su 11:00–18:00 | price=€10, concession €5; Kulturforum all exhibitions: €12, concessions €6 | wikipedia=Gemäldegalerie, Berlin | image=Berlin Kulturforum 2002a.jpg | wikidata=Q165631 |lastedit=2018-08-11| content=The Gemäldegalerie contains an astounding array of paintings, including works by Rembrandt, Rubens, Botticelli, Raphael, Titian, Goya, Velasquez and Watteau. The collection contains works from the old Bodemuseum on Museumsinsel in the East, now closed, and the former Gemäldegalerie in Dahlem. Its strong points are German paintings of the 13-16th centuries, Netherlandish painting of the 15th and 16th centuries, Flemish paintings of the 17th century, and miniature paintings of the 16th-19th centuries. In the newer section of the museum, designed by architects Heinz Hilmer and Christoph Sattler, there is enough space to display 1,150 masterpieces in the main gallery and 350 in the studio gallery - of the almost 2,900 pieces in the European painting collections. Established in 1830, the newly built gallery from 1998 has about 7,000 sq m of exhibition space (a complete tour of the 72 rooms covers almost 2 km). }} * {{see | name=Kunstgewerbemuseum | alt=Museum of Decorative Arts | url=http://www.smb.museum/en/museums-institutions/kunstgewerbemuseum/home.html | email= | address=Tiergartenstraße 6 | lat=52.5097 | long=13.3675 | directions=Visitor entrance from Matthäikirchplatz | phone=+49 20 266 2902 | tollfree= | fax=+49 20 266 2947 | hours=Tu-F 10:00-18:00, Sa Su 11:00-18:00 | price= | wikipedia=Kunstgewerbemuseum Berlin | image=Kulturforum 2007 5.jpg | wikidata=Q477503 | content=The oldest museum of its kind in Germany which, despite great losses during the World War II, still possesses one of the world's primary collections of European applied art. There are two sections to the collection: one located at the Kulturforum in Tiergarten, the other at Köpenick Palace. }} * {{see | name=Kupferstichkabinettt | alt=Museum of Prints and Drawings | url=http://www.smb.museum/museen-und-einrichtungen/kupferstichkabinett/home.html | email= | address=Matthäikirchplatz | lat=52.5084 | long=13.367 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu–F 10:00–18:00, Sa Su 11:00–18:00 | price=€6, concession €3; Kulturforum all exhibitions: €16, concessions €8 | wikipedia=Kupferstichkabinett Berlin | image=Berlin Kulturforum Kunstbibliothek und Kupferstichkabinett.jpg | wikidata=Q555946 | content=The largest collection of graphic art in Germany. }} * {{see | name=Neue Nationalgalerie | alt=New National Gallery | url=http://www.smb.museum/museen-und-einrichtungen/neue-nationalgalerie/home.html | email= | address=Potsdamer Straße 50 | lat=52.506806 | long=13.367917 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu W 10:00–18:00, Th 10:00–20:00 (from 16:00 free entrance), F–Su 10:00–18:00, M sed | price= | wikipedia=Neue Nationalgalerie | image=Neue Nationalgalerie Berlin 2004-02-21.jpg | wikidata=Q32659772 | content=Spectacular building by Mies van der Rohe contains its own collection and temporary exhibitions. }} * {{see | name=Documentation Centre for Displacement, Expulsion, Reconciliation | alt= | url=https://www.flucht-vertreibung-versoehnung.de/visitor-info-en | email=info@sfvv.de | address=Stresemannstraße 90, 10963 Berlin | lat= | long= | directions=near S-Bahn station Anhalter Bahnhof | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Tu-Su 10:00-19:00 | price=Free (ticket needs to be booked in advance on website) | lastedit=2022-08-07 | content=The Documentation Centre features exhibits, library and testimony archive about forced migration and expulsion of people in the 20th century. The main focus is on the fate of German refugees in the course of World War II, but that issue is presented in the context of the German attack on its neighbours in the war and the prior displacement and expulsion of other ethnic groups on the orders of the German government. The museum was controversial before it was even opened as it was championed by Erika Steinbach, a controversial right wing figure in the German expellee movement which is dominated by the political right. Steinbach and her idea of a "centre against expulsions" were particularly controversial in Poland, but the finished museum has seen no input from Steinbach and tries to strike a balance between portraying the suffering of expellees (German or otherwise) while also not ignoring the greater context and the fact that the expulsions were the direct result of German military aggression. }}
===Oranienburger Vorstadt===
* {{see | name=Berlin Wall Memorial | alt=Gedenkstätte Berliner Mauer | url=http://www.berliner-mauer-gedenkstaette.de/en | email= | address=Bernauer Straße 119 (visitor centre) and 111 (documentation centre) | lat=52.53374 | long=13.38753 | directions={{station|Bernauer Straße|U8}}, {{station|Nordbahnhof|S1|S2|S25}} or Tram M10 Gedenkstätte Berliner Mauer. Follow the signs in the stations – wall is Mauer in German | phone=+49 30 464-1030 | tollfree= | fax=+49 30 460 69 740 | hours=Memorial grounds daily 08:00–22:00. Visitor Centre and Documentation Centre: Tu–Su 10:00–18:00, M closed | price=Free; guided tour €3.50 | wikipedia=Gedenkstätte Berliner Mauer | image=Mémorial du mur de Berlin (6330924243).jpg | wikidata=Q95517 | content=The memorial site stretches along the full 1.5-km length of Bernauer Straße. The listing marker points to the '''visitor centre'''. Various monuments can be found along the entire length of the street, documenting nearby escape attempts and tunnels; captions are in German and English. The '''documentation centre''' across the street on Bernauer Straße/Ackerstraße is excellent (although most of the documentation is in German). The '''viewing platform''' next to the documentation centre gives you a tiny hint of the true scale of the Wall and how terrifying the "no man's land" between the two sections of walls must have been. The monument (that you can see from the platform) is a complete section of 4th generation wall - both inside and outside sections, and you can peer through from the east side to see the remains of the electric fence and anti-tank devices in the death strip. It really helps you understand what an incredible feat it was to get from one side to the other -- and why so many died doing it. The memorial site is often missed by tourists but an absolute must for anyone interested in this part of the city's history. It's a memorial to those who died crossing, so you won't, fortunately, get the tackiness of the Checkpoint Charlie area; instead you will be left with a haunting feeling of what life with the wall may have been really like.<br />Bernauer Straße is a street with a great deal of Wall history: it came to tragic prominence on August 13, 1961 when East German authorities closed the border and the street (with houses in the East but the street in the West). Border guards walled the doors and windows shut to keep Easterners from escaping by jumping out the window while Westerners (including police and fire brigades who brought life nets to help catch refugees) looked on in horror. The first recorded Wall-related death - the notorious Peter Fechter case (he bled to death in the "no-man's-land" with both sides unwilling or unable to help him) - was here, as was one of the famous tunnels and the famous photograph of the GDR border guard leaping over the barbed wire. }} * {{see | name=Kapelle der Versöhnung | alt=Chapel of Reconciliation | url=http://www.kapelle-versoehnung.de/bin/englisch/index.php | email= | address= | lat=52.535833 | long=13.391944 | directions=on the grounds of the Memorial Center | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Chapel of Reconciliation | image=La chapelle de la Réconciliation (Centre d'information du Mur, Berlin).jpg | wikidata=Q1728467 | lastedit=2018-03-01 | content=This chapel was built on the site of a church built in 1894 which sat on the "death strip" and was thus blown up by the GDR authorities in 1985. The chapel is the site of occasional memorial services for victims of the wall. }} * {{see | name=Museum für Naturkunde | alt=natural history museum | url=http://www.naturkundemuseum-berlin.de/en | email= | address=Invalidenstraße 43 | lat=52.5302 | long=13.3792 | directions=U-Bahn: U6 Naturkundemuseum, Tram: M6, M8, M10 | phone=+49 30 2093-8591 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=Tu–F 09:30–18:00, Sa Su 10:00–18:00 | price=€5, concession €3 | wikipedia=Museum für Naturkunde | image=Berlin, Mitte, Invalidenstrasse 43, Museum für Naturkunde.jpg | wikidata=Q233098 | content=More than 30 million objects in the scientific collection and a fascinating exhibition in one of the most significant institutions of its kind in the world. Some parts still under construction. }}
===Private art galleries===
As Berlin is a city of art, it is quite easy to find an art gallery on your way. They provide a nice opportunity to have a look at modern artists' work in a not so crowded environment for free. Some gallery streets in Mitte with more than about a dozen galleries are Auguststraße, Linienstraße, Torstraße, Brunnenstraße (all north of S-Bahn station Oranienburger Straße) and Zimmerstraße (U-Bahn station Kochstraße). A directory listing of all Mitte's art galleries can be found on [http://www.berolinart.com/mitte/ Berolin Art].
* {{see | name=Art Center Berlin Friedrichstraße | alt= | url=http://www.art-center-berlin.de | email= | address=Friedrichstraße 134 | lat=52.52339 | long=13.38751 | directions= | phone=+49 30 27879020 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Art_Center_Berlin | image=Art Center Berlin - before opening in June 2005.jpg | wikidata=Q705459 | content=Four floors of exhibitions with a relatively good variety of genres and artists. A very nice oasis of calm from the busy Friedrichstraße. }} * {{see | name=Galerie Eigen & Art | alt= | url=http://www.eigen-art.com | email= | address=Auguststraße 26 | lat=52.52697 | long=13.39715 | directions= | phone=+49 30 280 6605 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=One of the most famous German art galleries, home to the Neue Leipziger Schule (Neo Rauch et al.) }} * {{see | name=loop—raum fur aktuelle kunst | alt= | url=http://www.loop-raum.de | email= | address=Jägerstraße 5 | lat=52.513544 | long=13.385553 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Known for being the "incubator" of future famous Berlin artists. Primarily featuring sculpture video, and painting. }}
==Do==
=== Theater === Germany is one of the countries in the world where there is strong political consensus that "high culture" ought to be available even to those of little means, so publicly subsidized theaters abound. Naturally those are especially plentiful in the capital and the most famous ones are almost all in ''Mitte''. During the Weimar Republic Berlin was among the most innovative places in theater and many names still known to the average German in the 21st century were active in this theater scene. Some of their old stomping grounds survived the war or were rebuilt afterwards, so why not enjoy a Brecht piece the way Brecht wanted it staged at a theater Brecht worked at? [[File:Admiralspalast Berlin Friedrichstraße 1050-v4.jpg|thumb|Admiralspalast in the Friedrichstraße]] * {{do | name=Admiralspalast | alt=Admiral Palace | url=http://www.admiralspalast.de/ | email= | address=Friedrichstraße 101 | lat=52.5208 | long=13.3886 | directions=Train station Friedrichstraße | phone=+49 30-2250 7000,+49 30 4799 7499 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Admiralspalast | image=Berlin, Mitte, Friedrichstrasse 101-102, Admiralspalast 01.jpg | wikidata=Q359242 | content= }} * {{do | name=Berliner Ensemble | alt=Theater am Schiffbauer Damm | url=https://www.berliner-ensemble.de/ | email=theaterkasse@berliner-ensemble.de | address=Bertolt-Brecht-Platz 1 | lat=52.521667 | long=13.386111 | directions=Train station Friedrichstraße | phone=+49 30 284-08-155 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Berliner Ensemble | image=Berlin - Theater am Schiffbauerdamm, 2006.jpg | wikidata=Q534914 | content=Contemporary theatre. The theater was founded by Bertolt Brecht after the war in what was then East Berlin. }} * {{do | name=Chamäleon Theater Berlin | alt= | url=http://chamaeleonberlin.de/home | email= | address=Rosenthaler Straße 40 | lat=52.52399 | long=13.40220 | directions= | phone=+49 30 4000 5930 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Chamäleon (Varieté) | image=Hackesche Höfe (Berlin) 2.jpg | wikidata=Q1061549 | content=Located in the stunning Hackesche Höfe in the Berlin-Mitte quarter, the Chamaeleon Theatre offers exciting cross genre variety and music shows. }} * {{do | name=Deutsches Theater | alt=German Theatre | url=https://www.deutschestheater.de/en/ | email= | address=Schumannstraße 13a | lat=52.52426 | long=13.38193 | directions=U-Bahn Oranienburger Tor, Bus 147 | phone=+49 30 28441-221, +49 30 28441-225 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Deutsches Theater (Berlin) | image=Berlin, Mitte, Schumannstrasse 12-13A, Deutsches Theater und Kammerspiele 04.jpg | wikidata=Q703955 | content=Classical theater with impressive line up of actors and directors. Some events have English subtitles. }} * {{do | name=Kabarett Theater Distel | alt= | url=http://www.distel-berlin.de | email=distel@distel-berlin.de | address=Friedrichstraße 101 | lat=52.52070 | long=13.38856 | directions= | phone=+49 30 20 44 704 | tollfree= | fax=+49 30-20 81 555 | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Kabarett-Theater Distel | image=Berlin, Mitte, Friedrichstrasse, Admiralspalast 02.jpg | wikidata=Q1211965 | content=Cabaret and comedy, political satire in German. Their home venue is in the Admiralspalast. }} * {{do | name=Kunsthaus ACUD | alt=Free arts centre | url=http://www.acud.de/ | email= | address=Veteranenstraße 21 | lat=52.533397 | long=13.400965 | directions=U-Bahn: U8 Rosenthaler Platz, Tram: M8,12 Brunnenstr./Invalidenstr. | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:ACUD | image=Kunsthaus ACUD Berlin 2007.jpg | wikidata=Q288846 | content=The ACUD arts centre houses a theater, a gallery, two cinemas, a club, a concert venue, a bar and several artist's studios. }} * {{do | name=Maxim Gorki Theater | alt= | url=http://www.gorki.de/en | email=ticket@gorki.de | address=Am Festungsgraben 2 | lat=52.5189 | long=13.3951 | directions=Tram „Am Kupfergraben“, Bus 100, 200, TXL „Staatsoper“ | phone=+49 30 20221-115 (box office), +49 30 20221-129 (visitor service) | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Maxim Gorki Theater | image=Berlin, Mitte, Maxim-Gorki-Theater 02.jpg | wikidata=Q460259 | lastedit=2018-01-09 | content=The smallest municipal theatre in Berlin. It was an important venue for contemporary theatre in the German Democratic Republic (GDR). Nowadays its program continues with its political critique. Next to productions of classics, it has a big focus on new and contemporary writing and productions. It aims at being open to anyone regardless of their background. Sometimes plays the ''3 Pennys Opera'' by Brecht. }} * {{do | name=Volksbühne am Rosa-Luxemburg-Platz | alt=People's Theatre | url=https://www.volksbuehne.berlin/en/ | email=info@volksbuehne-berlin.de | address=Linienstraße 227 / Rosa-Luxemburg-Platz | lat=52.526944 | long=13.411944 | directions= | phone=+49 30 24065-5 (switchboard ), +49 30 24065-777 (customer service) | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Volksbühne | image=VolksbühBerlJan08.JPG | wikidata=Q617244 | content=Sometimes controversial, modern theater. }}
=== Opera and musicals === [[File:Berlin Friedrichstadtpalast Nacht 4831-v4.jpg|thumb|Revue theatre Friedrichstadt-Palast]] [[File:Berlin Opera UdL asv2018-05.jpg|thumb|Berlin State Opera house]] [[File:Berlin Theater am Potsdamer Platz.jpg|thumb|Musical Theater am Potsdamer Platz]] * {{do | name=Friedrichstadt-Palast | alt=Europe's Show Palace | url=https://www.palast.berlin/en | email=tickets@palast.berlin | address=Friedrichstraße 107 | lat=52.523908 | long=13.387939 | directions=U-Bahn Oranienburger Tor | phone=+49 30 2326 2326 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Tickets: €16.90-104.90 | wikipedia=Friedrichstadt-Palast | image=Friedrichstadtpalast-BLN.jpg | wikidata=Q565670 | content=Berlin's biggest show with over 100 artists on the biggest theater stage in the world. }} * {{do | name=Komische Oper Berlin | alt= | url=http://www.komische-oper-berlin.de/en | email= | address=Behrenstraße 55-57 | lat=52.51567 | long=13.38699 | directions= | phone=+49 30 47997400 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Komische Oper Berlin | image=Berlin, Mitte, Behrenstraße, Komische Oper 02.jpg | wikidata=Q687694 | lastedit=2018-01-23 | content=Founded in 1947 and at the forefront of modern opera. Their orchestra also performs regular concerts. Guided tours behind the scenes are offered. Each seat has subtitles where you can choose your language. }} * {{do | name=Staatsoper Unter den Linden | alt=Berlin State Opera | url=https://www.staatsoper-berlin.de/en/ | email= | address=Unter den Linden 7 | lat=52.51706 | long=13.39467 | directions= | phone=+49 30 20354 555 (tickets), +49 30 20354 0 (general enquiries) | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Berlin State Opera | image=Berlin, Mitte, Unter den Linden, Staatsoper 02.jpg | wikidata=Q312576 | content=The impressive building and royal history make the building alone worth a visit. The building reopened in December 2017 after extensive refurbishments and acoustic improvements. The general music director of the Staatsoper and its resident orchestra, Staatskapelle Berlin, is the conductor Daniel Barenboim. }} * {{do | name=Theater am Potsdamer Platz | alt= | url=https://www.stage-entertainment.de/unternehmen/theater-vermietung/stage-theater-am-potsdamer-platz.html | email= | address=Marlene-Dietrich-Platz 1 | lat=52.507261 | long=13.372182 | directions=Train station Potsdamer Platz | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=de:Theater am Potsdamer Platz | image=MJK33041 Viceroy's House (Berlinale 2017).jpg | wikidata=Q328706 | content=Musicaltheater and Berlinale (Berlin International Film Festival). }}
===Concerts=== * {{do | name=Berliner Philharmonie | alt= | url=http://www.berliner-philharmoniker.de/en/ | email=kartenbuero@berliner-philharmoniker.de | address=Herbert-von-Karajan-Str. 1 | lat=52.509987 | long=13.369642 | directions=Bus 200, M41: Philharmonie, or Bus M48, M85, N2: Varian-Fry-Str. | phone=+49 30 254 88 999 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Berliner Philharmonie | image=Berlin - Philharmonie Kulturforum Nacht.jpg | wikidata=Q32653910 | content=The Berliner Philharmonie is a concert hall with 2,440 seats in Berlin-Tiergarten (constructed from 1960 to 1963 and designed by Hans Scharoun) and the home of the '''Berlin Philharmonic Orchestra'''. It is a famous building with outstanding musicians. Reservations are recommended, but cheaper tickets are usually available 2–4 hours before the concert if not sold out. Every Tuesday (September to June) at 13:00–14:00, there is a free lunch concert; come early. In the winter, late-night concerts (22:30 or 23:00) are a bargain and often have more avant-garde or unconventional formats. The '''Kammermusiksaal''' (Chamber Music Hall) was added later in 1984–1987 as a venue for smaller concerts with 1,180 seats. It is linked to the foyer of the Philharmonie. Both buildings are located in the Kulturforum area. }} * {{do | name=Hochschule für Musik Hanns Eisler (HfM) | alt=Berlin Academy of Music | url=http://www.hfm-berlin.de/en/nc/events/events-calendar/ | email= | address=Neuer Marstall, Schloßplatz 7 | lat=52.51654 | long=13.40338 | directions= | phone=+49 30 688305700 | tollfree= | hours= | price=Many concerts are free | lastedit=2018-01-08 | content=The HfM offers many concerts by their students and other professional musicians. The concerts are held at various venues of the HfM, such as at Charlottenstr. 55, 10117 Berlin or at the address from this listing. }} * {{do | name=Konzerthaus Berlin | alt=Concert House Berlin | url=https://www.konzerthaus.de/en/ | email=kontakt@konzerthaus.de | address=Gendarmenmarkt | lat=52.513639 | long=13.392119 | directions=U2 Hausvogteiplatz or U6 Französische Straße | phone=+49 30 203092 101 (tickets ), +49 30 203092 333 (general enquiries) | tollfree= | fax=+49 30 203092 249 | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Konzerthaus Berlin | image=Berlin_Mitte_Konzerthaus_Gendarmenmarkt_Abend.jpg | wikidata=Q702548 | content= }}
===Cinema=== * During summertime you can enjoy an '''open-air cinema''' in front of the ''Altes Museum'', showing alternative movies (most of them in original language). It's very wise to buy tickets for the "Sommerkino" in the afternoon if you don't want to join a long queue at night with the chance of not getting a ticket. * {{do | name=Babylon | alt= | url=http://www.babylonberlin.de/ | email= | address=Rosa-Luxemburg-Str. 30 | lat=52.52589 | long=13.41143 | directions= | phone=+49 30 2425969 (from 17:00) | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=Movies are around €7-9; some movies are free | wikidata=Q1742238 | lastedit=2018-03-21 | content=Great cinema with a large program of non-mainstream movies. They have a working cinema organ, which is also played every Saturday for free midnight screenings of silent movies. }} * {{do | name=CineStar IMAX im Sony Center | alt= | url=http://www.cinestar.de/de/kino/cinestar-imax-im-sony-center/ | email=imax@cinestar.de | address=Potsdamer Straße 4 | lat=52.51004 | long=13.37278 | directions= | phone=+49 30 260 66 400 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | image=Berlin.Potzdamer Strasse 001.JPG | content=The worldwide recognised theatre at Potsdamer Straße }} * {{do | name=CineStar Original | alt= | url=https://www.cinestar.de/kino-berlin-original-im-sony-center/ | email= | address= | lat=52.509602 | long=13.373494 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The "CineStar Original" cinema located inside the Sony Center at the Potsdamer-Platz shows only movies in original version (e.g. in English, without subtitles). }} * {{do | name=Filmtheater Hackesche Höfe | alt= | url=http://www.hoefekino.de/content/english | email= | address=Rosenthaler Straße 40/41 | lat=52.524068 | long=13.402108 | directions=4th floor of the Hackesche Höfe (Hof 1); U Weinmeister Straße or U Hackescher Markt | phone=+49 30 2834603 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Very broad range of movies. }} * {{do | name=Kino Central | alt= | url=http://www.kino-central.de/kino.php | email= | address=Rosenthalerstraße 39 | lat=52.524252 | long=13.402572 | directions=near Hackesche Höfe; U Weinmeister Straße or U Hackescher Markt | phone=+49 30 28599973 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Repertory cinema located in an ex-squat. }}

They also have a branch near Checkpoint Charlie at Charlottenstraße. }} * {{eat | name=Midtown Grill | url=http://www.midtown-grill.de/ | email=restaurant@midtown-grill.de | address=Ebertstraße 3 | lat=52.51098 | long=13.37646 | directions= | phone=+49 30-22000 6415 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Following the tradition of the old American steakhouses, at Midtown Grill you will find the best steaks in town. }} * {{eat | name=Ständige Vertretung | alt=Permanent Mission | url=https://www.staev.de/berlin-mitte.html | email=info@staev.de | address=Schiffbauerdamm 8 | lat=52.5212 | long=13.3855 | directions= | phone=+49 30 2823965 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=daily 10:30-01:00 | price= | lastedit=2015-10-06 | content=Ständige Vertretung stands for Permanent Mission. They also have "all you can eat hot pot" deal for €12.80/person (minimum 2 people, 2015), which even though it lacks some ingredients you would normally get in a hot pot in Beijing (more variety of mushrooms - here you just get champignons - tofu skin, etc), still tastes as it should taste. === Around Klosterstraße ===
==== Mid range ==== * {{eat | name=The Greens - Coffee & Plants | alt= | url=http://www.the-greens-berlin.de | email= | address=Am Krögel 2 | lat=52.5152 | long=13.4091 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata= | lastedit=2022-08-05 | content=Stylish café located in an old warehouse filled with plants, most of them for sale. }} * {{eat | name=Zur letzten Instanz | alt= | url=https://www.zurletzteninstanz.com | email= | address=Waisenstraße 14-16 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+49 30 2425528 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikidata=Q230377 | lastedit=2022-08-05 | content=Often claimed to be Berlin's oldest restaurant. While there has been an establishment at the site since 1621, the current name dates back to 1924. What's more is that much of the original building was damaged in World War II and reconstructed in 1963 by the East German authorities to make it more "appealing to tourists". Still, Zur letzten Instanz serves excellent, albeit a bit price, German cuisine. In the backyard, there's a nice beer garden. }}
==Drink==
===Cafés=== [[File:St_oberholz.jpg|thumb|Café Sankt Oberholz]] * {{drink | name=Barcomi's Deli | alt= | url=http://www.barcomis.de/index.php | email= | address=Sophie-Gips-Höfe, 2. Hof, Sophienstr. 21 | lat=52.52615 | long=13.40087 | directions=S Hackescher Markt or U Weinmeisterstraße | phone= | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 09:00-21:00, Su 10:00-21:00 | price= | content=A deli with superb American cakes and cookies run by the American Cynthia Barcomi. Apple walnut caramel cake, Devil's Food cake, Lemon Meringue Pie, Triple Chocolate Cookies, they have it all. There are also bagels, salads and lasagna on the menu for the non sweet-tooths. You can try to repeat the wonder at home with her four recipe books. They also roast their own coffee which is excellent as well. Not so easy to find though - it is in a courtyard of a building situated roughly in the middle of the quiet Sophienstraße and there is only a small sign outside. Maybe look it up on Google Maps before visiting. The smaller original branch is situated at Bergmannstraße in Kreuzberg. }} * {{drink | name=The Barn | alt= | url=http://barn.bigcartel.com/coffee-shop | email= | address=Augusstraße 58| lat=52.52744 | long=13.39814 | directions=S Oranienburger Straße | phone=| tollfree= | fax= | hours=08:00–18:00 | price= | content=A small cafe that takes coffee very seriously. Or, to say it in their words: "Please be aware that we have certain preferences when serving a coffee the way we believe it tastes best." Children are not really welcome, there is only a restricted area for laptops. Good sandwiches. }} * {{drink|name=Café am Neuen See|lat=52.51036 | long=13.34449|content=A must-do during the day if the weather is nice. This cafe is more like an outdoor beergarden. Even though it's right in the middle of West Berlin, the "Café am Neuen See" is located in the middle of the Tiergarten next to a small lake. If you are a (romantic) couple, make sure to rent one of the rowboats and enjoy the silent and romantic scenery. The nicer the weather, the more packed this place gets. Don't even dream of a place to sit if it's one of the rare, warm Sundays.|url=http://www.cafe-am-neuen-see.de/|price=Beers are between €4-5, giant pizzas are €8-9}} * {{drink | name=Café Fleury| alt= | url=| email= | address=Weinbergsweg 20 | lat=52.53121 | long=13.40224 | directions=S Rosenthaler Platz | phone=| tollfree= | fax= | hours=M-F 08:00–20:00, Sa Su 10:00-20:00| price=from €3 | content=Cute little French cafe near Weinbergspark. Excellent breakfast and tasty baguettes. Tends to be very crowded on weekends, be there when it opens if you want to have breakfast. If it is full, try the small sister Petit Fleury on the opposite side of the road. }} * {{drink | name=Cafe Solvey| alt= | url=http://www.cafesolvey.de/| email= | address=Elisabethkirchstraße 1 | lat=52.53274 | long=13.39785 | directions=S Rosenthaler Platz | phone=+49 30-67961222| tollfree= | fax= | hours= Tu-F 12:00-18:00; Sa-Su 10:00-19:00| price=from €3 | content=Lovely little vintage cafe. While drinks and cakes are decent, but not extraordinary for Mitte, the decor is really cute and it is very quiet. A good place to relax and read a magazine or book. }} * {{drink | name=Dachgartenrestaurant Käfer | alt= | url= | email= | address=Platz der Republik 1 | lat=52.51860 | long=13.37666 | directions=U Bundestag or S Brandenburger Tor | phone=+49 30 22 62 99 0 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Breakfast from 09:00-10:30 at the top of the Germany's parliament. You have to contact them in advance and need to show your ID, since the entrance is through the parliament building. }} * {{drink | name=Ebe Ano | alt= | url=http://www.ebe-ano.de | email= | address=Pohl Straße 52 | lat=52.500880 | long=13.364331 | directions= | phone=+49 3047384590 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | content=Nigerian soul food }} * {{drink | name=Kauf Dich Glücklich | alt= | url=http://www.kaufdichgluecklich-shop.de/berlin | email= | address=Kastanienallee 54 | lat=52.533385 | long=13.404188 | directions=U-Bahn: U8 Rosenthaler Platz, Tram: M1,M8,12 Zionskirchplatz | phone=+49 30 48623348 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=10:00/11:00–20:00 | price= | content=The café with its iconic colored chairs on the terrace which at the same time is a shop for fashion and design. They also offer coworking spaces. | image=St_oberholz.jpg }} * {{drink | name=Strandbad Mitte | alt= | url= | email= | address=Kleine Hamburger Straße 16 (access through Augusstraße) | lat=52.52734 | long=13.39666 | directions=S Oranienburger Straße. | phone=+49 30 24 62 89 63 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price=from €5 | content=Good breakfast and playground next to the restaurant. }} * {{drink | name=Telecafé | alt= | url= | email= | address=Panoramastraße 1a | lat= | long= | directions=S and U Alexanderplatz | phone=+49 30 242 33 33 | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=Enjoy breakfast in front of a city view right at the top of the Fernsehturm. }} * {{drink | name=Zimt und Zucker | alt= | url=http://www.zimtundzucker.com/ | email= | address=Schiffbauerdamm 12 | lat=52.52061 | long=13.38429 | directions=S/U Friedrichstraße (there is a direct access to Schiffsbauerdamm from the S Bahn track 5/7/75 | phone=+49 30 81010858 | tollfree= | fax= | hours=daily 09:30-21:00 | price= | content=A small and lively cafe in the style of the 20s. There is a terrace on the river in summer. Their German cheesecake (with Quark and therefore lighter than the American version) comes especially recommended. }}

Has a very industrial ambience, ceramic tiles on the wall, a small dance floor, and a good sound system. | image= }} * {{drink | name=Tresor | alt= | url=http://tresorberlin.com/ | email= | address=Köpenicker Str. 70 | lat=52.510788 | long=13.419809| directions=S-Bahn: S3,S5,S7,S75 Jannowitzbrücke, U-Bahn: U8 Heinrich-Heine-Straße, Bus: 165,265,N8,N65 | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | content=The history of this club dates back to 1990s and the start of techno/house scene in Berlin. The old venue in the vault space of the former Wertheim department store in Leipziger Strasse was closed in 2005, but Tresor reopened in May 2007 in a decommissioned power plant in the southeast of Berlin-Mitte. |image=Tresor-Logo.svg |wikipedia=Tresor | wikidata=Q678186}} * {{drink | name=Clärchens Ballhaus | alt= | url=https://www.claerchensballhausberlin.de/ | email= | address=Auguststraße 24 | lat=52.5269 | long=13.3967 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | hours=Daily from 11:00 usually to 00:00 on weeknights, and around 04:00 on weekends); hot food 12:00-22:30, garden closes 23:00, dance starts 21:00 | price=Entrance fee depending on event: usually free on weeknights, €5 on weekends | wikidata=Q1103846 | lastedit=2019-07-21 | content=Traditional dance hall and restaurant established 1913. One of the few surviving Berlin ballrooms from the pre-WW1 era, featuring a hall of mirrors. Primarily known for its dance events (be it Disco, Swing, Salsa, Tango, Cha-Cha or "Schwoof", i.e. freestyle), it also hosts concerts, but you may as well just come for dinner or a drink. The venue has been a set for films like ''Inglourious Basterds'' and ''Stauffenberg''. }}
==Sleep== Accommodation in Mitte mostly caters for the backpacker or business traveller so the mid-range market is small. When you intend to travel for a trade fair, prices tend to rise fast but not as bad as in [[Frankfurt]]. During off-peak times, the splurge hotels offer substantial discounts that bring down the price to mid-range level (€120 per night is offered sometimes), so check carefully upfront for special offers.

==Connect==
==Go next==
{{usabledistrict}} {{IsPartOf|Berlin}} {{geo|52.519444|13.406667}}

{{pagebanner|Berlin Reinickendorf banner.jpg|caption=Schäfersee lake in Berlin-Reinickendorf}} The northwest of Berlin consists of the boroughs of '''Spandau''' and '''Reinickendorf''' was urbanized in the 19th century. Before that, it largely consisted of small towns. In the early 20th century, it was the working-class area with heavy industry where Siemens was founded; indeed, part of Reinickendorf is called Siemensstadt. The area is more residential today, though big industrial complexes remain.
==Understand== '''Spandau''' is the westernmost borough (''Bezirk'') of the [[Germany|German]] capital city of [[Berlin]]. It is a green area near the Spree and Havel rivers. Once a town in its own right, Spandau is one of the oldest areas in the Berlin region and retains much of its unique character, having been spared the worst of the Allied bombing in the Second World War that so devastated the rest of the city. The center of the district is formed by a dense network of medieval streets and a market square, retaining a large number of timber-framed buildings. Spandau was the site of the military prison in which Hitler's deputy Rudolf Hess was imprisoned after the 1946 Nuremberg trials until his death in 1987. The prison was then demolished. Despite being part of Berlin, neither Berliners nor Spandauers usually consider that fact to apply in popular thought. If for example, you ask a taxi driver to take you to the "city center", they will most likely take you to the center of Spandau, not necessarily [[Berlin/Mitte|Mitte]] or [[Berlin/City West|the old heart of West Berlin]]. It's as much in the boondocks as some Berliners ever venture, and even those who consider Potsdam in BVG zone C to be at the edge of civilization might consider Spandau basically another planet.
'''[http://www.reinickendorf.de/ Reinickendorf]''' is a borough in the northwest of Berlin. It consists of the localities Tegel, Reinickendorf, Märkisches Viertel (1970s-era apartment blocks area) and some small village-like areas. Reinickendorf loves to use fox symbolism as "Reinicke" is a poetic term for fox (the standard term is "Fuchs") which is used in fables. The coat of arms thus shows a fox, and Berlin's most successful handball team, Füchse Berlin, is from here though they now play their home games in Max Schmeling Halle in the former east.
In general, it is a very green district with much water, and the neighborhoods have a typical small town cityscape. There are some minor sights interesting for tourists (especially architecture in Tegel). Within the borough of Reinickendorf, Märkisches Viertel (sometimes derisively called "merkwüdiges Viertel" - peculiar quarter) is one of the most notable West Berlin new constructions, dating to the 1960s when housing was scarce. When the housing units were constructed, a subway connection was promised, but it took until the 1980s to extend U8 towards the area (by then in questionable parallelity to the S-Bahn) - and Berlin ran out of money just about one or two stops before actually reaching the neighborhood, causing considerable frustration among residents. The extension of U8 to the center of Märkisches Viertel is one of the most talked about U-Bahn extensions whenever proposals to extend the network are debated, and the Bezirk Reinickendorf is among those clamoring the loudest.
While both areas might feel almost "rural" in parts to Berliners, Spandau alone would be the largest city in Brandenburg were it not part of Berlin, so the "ruralness" of this district is a very relative term.
==Get in== {{Mapframe|52.5433|13.2500|height=560|width=410|zoom=11}} {{Mapshape|wikidata=Q158083, Q158876}} {{Mapshapes|Q68646}} <!-- U-bahn lines --> {{Mapshapes|Q99654}} <!-- Berlin S-Bahn --> {{Mapshape|wikidata=Q525056|type=geoline|stroke=#333333|stroke-width=5|stroke-opacity=0.7}} <!-- Ringbahn -->
Spandau is the terminal station for a couple of public transport lines (especially the backbone line {{rint|berlin|U7}}). Some long distance and all regional trains stop at Spandau.
===By public transport=== The main backbone for the western parts is the U-Bahn line {{rint|berlin|U7}}, Berlin's longest, with the major stations {{marker|type=go | name={{station|Zitadelle}} | lat=52.537778 | long=13.217778 | wikipedia=Zitadelle (Berlin U-Bahn) | image=U-Bahn Berlin U7 Zitadelle 1.JPG | wikidata=Q663212}}, {{marker|type=go | name={{station|Altstadt Spandau}} | lat=52.538889 | long=13.206389 | wikipedia=Altstadt Spandau (Berlin U-Bahn) | image=| wikidata=Q571402}} and {{marker|type=go | name={{station|Rathaus Spandau.}} | wikipedia=Rathaus Spandau (Berlin U-Bahn)| image=Train station Berlin Spandau 2.JPG | wikidata=Q650704}} The S-Bahn lines {{rint|berlin|S3}} and {{rint|berlin|S75}} end/start here as well.
The BVG (the public transport company) runs a public ferry between Kladow and Wannsee which is primarily designed for commuters.
The main U-Bahn routes for Reinickendorf are {{rint|berlin|U8}} for the more easterly parts and {{rint|berlin|U6}} for the more westerly parts.
<!--===By plane=== * {{go | name=Tegel Airport | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.559722 | long=13.287778 | directions=in the southwest of Reinickendorf | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Berlin Tegel Airport | image=Berlin-Tegel from the air.jpg | wikidata=Q9688 | content=Tegel Airport shut down on November 8 2020. Due to legal and safety reasons, Tegel will be held operational for air traffic for another six months, without handling any scheduled services, before being decomissioned as an aviation facility on 3 May 2021. The minor military area on the northern side of Tegel will still be used for governmental helicopter flights until 2029; the main terminal will be taken over by a university of applied sciences. }}-->
===By train=== [[File:Bahnhof Berlin-Spandau ICE1.jpg|thumb|Train station Berlin-Spandau with ICE]] * {{go | name=Berlin-Spandau | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.5347 | long=13.1958 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Berlin-Spandau station | image=Train station Berlin Spandau 2.JPG | wikidata=Q654980 | content=is a stop for many long-distance trains and all regional trains. It has interchanges to various S-Bahn, U-Bahn and buses. }}
==Get around== Both Spandau and Reinickendorf cover a pretty large area and as they are rather outlying, the U-Bahn is only of limited use. The S-Bahn meanwhile has stops too far apart to be useful for local transport in most cases. Unfortunately, there is not yet any tram in the area, the West Berlin tram having been shut down in the 1960s. For the most part, you'll thus have to rely on buses for your public transit needs. As elsewhere in Berlin a leading "M" in the line-number means "Metrobus" and they are more frequent, and also run at night but they can be crowded during peak times. Buses with a leading "X" in the line number meanwhile are express buses that skip some stops and will hopefully arrive a bit faster than the "regular" bus. An infamous phenomenon on busier Berlin bus routes is "bunching" when despite nominal five minute headway no bus comes for ten or even fifteen minutes as the first bus gets delayed by boarding and alighting passengers and the following buses catch up. This will lead to the strange experience of three buses arriving all at once the last notably emptier than the first one. The red-red-green government elected 2016 would love to replace the busiest bus lines with tram lines which don't show this problem but funds and planning capacity are limited and it can take years from the first idea to build a line to trams running.
==See==
===Spandau=== {{infobox|Siemensstadt|Siemens has a long and storied connection to Berlin and the neighbourhood of Spandau that officially bears the name "Siemensstadt" ("Siemens-City") was built in the early 20th century as a production site and as a representative showcase of one of Germany's leading technology companies. Siemens even had its own railway built in the 1920s - served by regular S-Bahn trains - in no small part aided by members of the Siemens family sitting on the board of ''Deutsche Reichsbahn''. However, after the unprecedented destruction in Berlin during World War II and partition a distinct possibility, Siemens began to search for new production sites and headquarters. The first site in [[Hof]] was abandoned because it was too close to the Soviet zone and Czechoslovakia). Sites in [[Munich]] later in [[Erlangen]] were chosen instead of Hof. The S-Bahn was boycotted in the West starting from the construction of the Berlin Wall and after a strike in 1980, the "Siemens railway" was shut down as it had now seemingly outlived its usefulness. However, the city decided to take over the infrastructure and put it under historic site protection. In 2018 Siemens signed a contract with Berlin returning to its former site in Siemensstadt. The reconstruction of the Siemensbahn gave Berlin a lot of high tech jobs, and the company got a representative headquarters and "Innovation Campus" in line with the 21st century.}} * {{see | name=Spandau Citadel | alt=Zitadelle Spandau | url=http://www.zitadelle-berlin.de/en/ | email= | address=Am Juliusturm 64 | lat=52.541389 | long=13.212222 | directions={{rint|Berlin|U7}} Zitadelle, Bus X33 (stop Zitadelle) | phone=+49 354 94 42 00 | tollfree= | hours= Tu-F 09:00-17:00, Sa Su 10:00-17:00 | price=entry €4.50 (concessions available) | wikipedia=Spandau Citadel | image=Zitadelle-Spandau-Torhaus groß.JPG | wikidata=Q206211 | lastedit=2018-08-25 | content=A fortress built between 1560 and 1590 to Italian design on the site of a 12th-century castle. The ''Juliusturm'' housed part of the huge indemnity (in physical gold coins) France had to pay after the war of 1870/71 until what was left of it was returned to France after Germany's loss in [[First World War|World War I]]. The term "Juliusturm" remained in usage in German for a large "rainy day fund" into the 1960s. Museums housed within the citadel cover the history of the town of Spandau, monumental public art in Berlin, and artillery. }} * {{see | name=St Nikolai Kirche | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.5384 | long=13.2053 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | image=Spandau Nikolaikirche Turm.jpg | wikidata=Q2316617 | content=A splendid 15th-century Gothic church with many fine accoutrements. }} * {{see | name=Militärhistorisches Museum der Bundeswehr – Flugplatz Berlin-Gatow | alt=Military History Museum; RAF Gatow | url=http://www.mhm-gatow.de/de | email= | address=Flugplatz Gatow 33 | lat=52.4731 | long=13.1447 | directions=from S+U Rathaus Spandau, take Bus 135 | phone= | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Militärhistorisches Museum Flugplatz Berlin-Gatow | image=2012-08 Luftwaffenmuseum Berlin-Gatow anagoria 06.JPG | wikidata=Q461359 | lastedit=2016-10-04 | content=At a former Luftwaffe and Royal Air Force (RAF) airfield, RAF Gatow. The museum's focus is on military history, particularly the history of the Luftwaffe of the Bundeswehr, with a collection of more than 200,000 items, including 155 aeroplanes, 5,000 uniforms and 30,000 books. There are also displays (including aeroplanes) on the history of the airfield when it was used by the RAF. Aircraft include reproductions of Otto Lilienthal's gliders, of World War I planes such as the Fokker E.III, and World War II planes such as the Bf 109 and Me-262, as well as at least one aircraft of every type ever to serve in the air forces of East and West Germany. Most of those postwar aircraft are stored outside on the tarmac and runways, however, and many are in bad condition. There are long term restoration projects, including a Focke-Wulf Fw 190. RAF Gatow is notable as the "missing third airport" of West Berlin. Each sector used to have its own airport; the French sector had Tegel, the American sector had Tempelhof and the British had Gatow. However, RAF Gatow never saw much traffic of any kind, was more kept as a political statement than for transportation value, and thus was shut down after reunification. Tempelhof, after having been shut down for civilian traffic from 1975 to 1981, closed for all flights in 2008, while Tegel shut down in November 2020. }}

The area houses now offices, a shopping mall and some places for entertainment. The company Borsig has a very turbulent and storied history and while it has long since ceased being a "German" company or connected to the family of the same name, the investors that bought the name and assets after a bankruptcy keep producing in Berlin-Tegel. }} * {{see | name=Alt Lübars | alt= | url= | email= | address= | lat=52.62072 | long=13.35750 | directions= | phone= | tollfree= | fax= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Lübars | image=Berlin Lübars Panaroma Tegeler Fließ.jpg | wikidata=Q563664 | content=One of the most authentic and oldest villages (1247) in the outskirts of Berlin, it looks the same way it did some hundred years ago. Take S-Bahn 1 to Waidmannslust and then bus 222 to Alt-Lübars. }}
